blurb: At every table where bread is broken and the cup is shared, time folds into eternity. These words invite you to taste anew the night when Love set his face toward the cross and still chose to give himself away. Let the simple elements become mirrors: as often as you eat and drink, you proclaim—not with eloquence speeches but with surrendered hearts—that a life laid down is the only food that truly feeds the world. Come hungry; leave bread for others.

verses.text: For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took bread:
And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in remembrance of me.
After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
For as often as ye eat this bread, and drink this cup, ye do shew the Lord’s death till he come.
